walkthrough.title: Choose Roles
walkthrough.text: Welcome to Mysterium! Before we set anything up, let's decide who will play the ghost. The ghost has a unique job: they cannot speak during the game and must communicate only through abstract vision cards. Everyone else will be a psychic. For your first game, it helps to have the most experienced player take the ghost role, since they need to understand how all the cards work.

walkthrough.title: Set the Difficulty
walkthrough.text: Next, let's pick a difficulty level. This determines how many suspect, location, and object cards we lay out on the table. Easy uses fewer cards, which makes it simpler to narrow down the right answer. Medium and hard add more options to sort through. For your first game, we strongly recommend easy.

walkthrough.title: Lay Out the Progress Boards and Clock
walkthrough.text: Place the four progress boards in a row where everyone can see them: character, location, object, and epilogue. Then set the clock board nearby and point the hour hand to the Roman numeral one. The clock tracks how many rounds we have left. We get seven hours total before the connection with the ghost is lost.

walkthrough.title: Deal Psychic Cards to the Table
walkthrough.text: Now let's set up the suspect lineup. Shuffle the character psychic cards face down, then draw the number shown on the difficulty table for your player count. Lay them face up in a row near the character progress board. Repeat this for the location and object psychic cards, placing each row near its matching progress board. These are the options the psychics will be choosing from during the game.

walkthrough.title: Ghost Sets Up the Screen
walkthrough.text: The ghost takes the game screen and sets it up so the inside faces only them. For each psychic card drawn, find the matching ghost card with the same number. Pass all the matching ghost cards to the ghost. The ghost shuffles them and secretly draws one character, one location, and one object card per psychic, placing each card in the correct column and row behind the screen. These hidden combinations are what the psychics need to figure out.

walkthrough.title: Prepare Vision Cards
walkthrough.text: Place the entire deck of eighty-four vision cards behind the ghost's screen. The ghost draws seven vision cards to form their starting hand. These beautifully surreal cards are the ghost's only way to communicate with the psychics. The ghost will use them to send abstract clues throughout the game.

walkthrough.title: Distribute Psychic Components
walkthrough.text: Each psychic picks a color and takes the matching intuition token, sleeve, and clairvoyancy level marker. Place each clairvoyancy marker on the zero space of the clairvoyancy track. If you have four or five players, each psychic also receives four numbered clairvoyancy tokens. With six or seven players, each psychic gets six tokens instead. With two or three players, skip the clairvoyancy tokens entirely.

walkthrough.title: Place Tokens and Start
walkthrough.text: Each psychic places their intuition token on the character progress board, since we always start by identifying suspects. The ghost places the matching colored ghost tokens behind the screen at the bottom of each psychic's column as a reminder. Set the sand timer and the crow markers for your difficulty level near the ghost. We are ready to begin the seance!

summary.title: Choose Roles
bullets: 1 player = ghost (silent communicator via vision cards)
All other players = psychics (interpreters)
Experienced player as ghost recommended for first game

bullets: Easy/Medium/Hard determines number of suspect/location/object cards
More cards = harder deduction
Easy recommended for first game

Set clock board with hour hand on I
7 hours total before the game is lost

Lay face-up near character progress board

Ghost draws 1 character + 1 location + 1 object per psychic

4-5 players: 4 clairvoyancy tokens each; 6-7 players: 6 tokens each
2-3 players: no clairvoyancy tokens (use variant rules)

summary.title: Place Tokens and Start
bullets: Psychics place intuition tokens on character progress board
Ghost places colored ghost tokens behind screen
Set sand timer and crow markers near ghost

walkthrough.title: The Two Phases
walkthrough.text: Mysterium is played in two phases. In Phase one, Reconstruction of Events, the psychics work to identify their assigned suspect, location, and weapon. This phase lasts up to seven hours on the clock. If all psychics finish in time, we move to Phase two, Revealing the Culprit, where the ghost sends one final shared vision and the psychics vote on who did it. If the majority votes correctly, everyone wins together!

walkthrough.title: How the Ghost Sends Visions
walkthrough.text: Each hour begins with the ghost sending visions. The ghost picks one or more vision cards from their hand and places them face up in front of a psychic. These cards are abstract and surreal, so the connection might be a color, a shape, a mood, or a tiny detail in the artwork. After giving cards to one psychic, the ghost pushes that psychic's ghost token against the screen as a reminder. Then the ghost draws back up to seven cards and repeats for the next psychic until everyone has received at least one vision card.

walkthrough.title: Interpreting Visions
walkthrough.text: Once all psychics have their vision cards, the discussion begins. Psychics can freely examine their own cards and the cards laid out on the table, and they should talk openly about what they see. Look for matching colors, similar objects, emotional tones, or any visual link between the vision card and the suspects, locations, or objects on the table. When the ghost finishes distributing all visions, they flip the sand timer. Each psychic must place their intuition token on the card they think the ghost is pointing to before time runs out.

walkthrough.title: Clairvoyancy Tokens
walkthrough.text: While the timer is running, psychics can also place clairvoyancy tokens on other psychics' guesses. Each token has an agree side and a disagree side. If you think another psychic chose correctly, place a token agree-side-up next to their intuition token. If you think they are wrong, place it disagree-side-up. You cannot bet on your own guess, and you can place only one token per other psychic per round. All used tokens are discarded to the clock board and returned at the start of hour four.

walkthrough.title: The Ghost Reveals Results
walkthrough.text: When the sand timer runs out, the ghost reveals whether each psychic guessed correctly. If a psychic was right, they collect the correct psychic card, slide it into their sleeve, discard all their vision cards, and advance their intuition token to the next progress board. If a psychic was wrong, they keep their vision cards in front of them and stay on the same progress board. Next turn, the ghost will add more vision cards on top of the existing ones to help them.

walkthrough.title: Clairvoyancy Track Progress
walkthrough.text: After each reveal, psychics who bet correctly on the clairvoyancy tokens advance one space on the clairvoyancy track. If you placed an agree token on a correct guess, you advance. If you placed a disagree token on an incorrect guess, you also advance. This track matters because it determines how many of the final shared vision cards you get to see before voting in Phase two. Higher clairvoyancy means more information in the finale.

walkthrough.title: Advancing the Clock
walkthrough.text: After all results are revealed, advance the hour hand one step on the clock. If any psychic has not finished identifying all three of their cards by the end of hour seven, the team loses immediately. When a psychic does finish all three, they place their intuition token on the mission accomplished space and gain one clairvoyancy track space for each hour remaining on the clock. They continue helping other psychics interpret visions for the rest of the game.

walkthrough.title: Ghost Discard and Crow Markers
walkthrough.text: Sometimes the ghost's hand of vision cards just does not have anything useful. The ghost can discard their entire hand and draw seven new cards, but this costs a crow marker. On easy difficulty, the ghost can do this once per turn. On medium, three times total for the entire game. On hard, just once for the entire game. Use this power wisely, because a bad hand at a critical moment with no crows left can cost the team the game.

walkthrough.title: Phase Two: Suspect Lineup
walkthrough.text: If all psychics finish Phase one in time, we move to the finale. Each psychic removes their three solved cards from their sleeve and groups them together. Place numbered ghost tokens next to each group. These groups represent the suspects. In a two or three player game, randomly create two extra groups from leftover psychic cards to bring the total to four groups.

walkthrough.title: Phase Two: Shared Vision
walkthrough.text: The ghost selects three vision cards from their hand to form a shared vision. One card should point to the character in the culprit's group, one to the location, and one to the object. The ghost shuffles these three cards and places them face down in the center of the table. Then the ghost secretly places the matching culprit token face down on the epilogue board. The psychics do not know which card corresponds to which element.

walkthrough.title: Phase Two: Straw Poll
walkthrough.text: Now the shared vision cards are revealed one at a time, based on clairvoyancy levels. Psychics at a low level see only the first card before voting. Those at an intermediate level see two cards before voting. Those at a high level see all three cards before voting. Each psychic secretly slides a numbered clairvoyancy token into their sleeve to cast their vote. The group with the most votes is the team's accusation.

walkthrough.title: Winning and Losing
walkthrough.text: Reveal all votes and compare the majority choice to the culprit token. If they match, everyone wins and the ghost's soul is laid to rest! If there is a tie, the group chosen by the psychic furthest along the clairvoyancy track breaks the tie. If still tied, the eldest tied player's choice wins. If the accused group does not match the culprit token, the team loses and the mystery remains unsolved. Remember, this is a cooperative game. We all win together or we all lose together.

summary.title: The Two Phases
bullets: Phase 1: Reconstruction — psychics identify suspect + location + object (max 7 hours)
Phase 2: Revealing the Culprit — shared vision + majority vote
Cooperative: all win or all lose together

walkthrough.title: Welcome to the Seance
walkthrough.text: In this practice tutorial, we will walk through a scripted opening with five players. Laura is the ghost, and four psychics are playing: Stephanie as blue, Paul as white, Carl as red, and Alex as yellow. Follow along even if your group is a different size. By the end, you will have seen the core loop of sending visions, interpreting them, and advancing through the investigation.

walkthrough.title: The Board Is Set
walkthrough.text: We are playing on medium difficulty with five players, so seven character cards, seven location cards, and seven object cards are laid face up on the table. Laura has secretly placed one character, one location, and one object behind her screen for each psychic. The clock is at hour one and every psychic's intuition token sits on the character progress board. Let's begin.

walkthrough.title: Hour One: The Ghost Sends Visions
walkthrough.text: Laura looks at Alex's hidden character card: the governess, whose artwork features a ball of red wool. She searches her hand and finds a vision card showing tangled red cords and another showing a woman in a hat. She places both cards face up in front of Alex. Then she pushes Alex's ghost token against the screen, draws back to seven cards, and moves on to Stephanie.

walkthrough.title: Alex Interprets the Vision
walkthrough.text: Alex looks at the two vision cards and scans the seven character cards on the table. The red cords remind him of the governess's wool, and the woman in the hat seems to match too. But another character card shows a similar shade of red in a different context. Alex is torn between two options. The other psychics chime in with their opinions, helping Alex think through the visual connections.

walkthrough.title: The Beginner Mistake: Literal Thinking
walkthrough.text: Carl receives a vision card showing a lush garden scene. He immediately looks for a character card with a garden in it and finds nothing close. He is stuck. Here is the mistake: Carl is reading the vision too literally. The ghost often communicates through color, mood, or small details, not the main subject of the image. Carl should look at the dominant green color, the peaceful feeling, or maybe a tiny object hidden in the corner. Let's help Carl think more abstractly.

walkthrough.title: Recovering from Literal Thinking
walkthrough.text: Carl steps back and looks at the vision card again with fresh eyes. Instead of looking for a garden on a character card, he notices the card has a prominent clock in the background. One of the character cards features a watchmaker. That could be the connection! Carl places his intuition token on the watchmaker. Whether he is right or wrong, he learned to look beyond the obvious subject of the vision card.

walkthrough.title: Clairvoyancy Bets
walkthrough.text: Before the sand timer runs out, Stephanie looks at Alex's guess and agrees. She places her clairvoyancy token agree-side-up next to Alex's intuition token. Paul disagrees with Carl's guess and places a disagree token. These bets cost nothing and advance your clairvoyancy track when you are right. A common beginner mistake is hoarding these tokens and never using them. Use your clairvoyancy tokens early and often because higher clairvoyancy gives you more information in the finale.

walkthrough.title: The Ghost Reveals Results
walkthrough.text: Time is up! Laura reveals the results one by one. Alex guessed correctly, so he collects the governess card, slides it into his sleeve, discards his vision cards, and moves his intuition token to the location progress board. Carl guessed wrong. He keeps his vision cards in front of him and stays on the character progress board. Next hour, Laura will give him additional vision cards on top of his existing ones to help narrow it down.

walkthrough.title: The Beginner Mistake: Ignoring Accumulated Clues
walkthrough.text: It is now hour two. Carl receives a new vision card from Laura. He looks only at the new card and ignores the garden card from last round that is still sitting in front of him. This is a classic beginner trap. When you guess wrong, your old vision cards stay because they are still valid clues. The ghost is building a multi-card story, and each new card adds to the picture. Always consider all your vision cards together as a combined message.

walkthrough.title: Discussing as a Team
walkthrough.text: Stephanie has already identified her character and is now working on locations. But she notices Carl is still stuck on characters. Instead of focusing only on her own puzzle, she leans over to look at all of Carl's vision cards together. She spots a pattern Carl missed. In Mysterium, helping each other is essential. Solved psychics should actively assist those who are behind, because if even one psychic fails to finish by hour seven, everyone loses.

walkthrough.title: The Beginner Mistake: Ghost Gives Too Many Cards
walkthrough.text: Laura is worried about Carl falling behind, so she gives him four vision cards at once in hour three. The problem: too many cards can overwhelm a psychic with conflicting information. Some details in different cards may point to different suspects, creating confusion rather than clarity. One or two carefully chosen vision cards are usually more effective than a pile of cards that muddy the message.

walkthrough.title: Mid-Game Clock Check
walkthrough.text: We are at hour four. The clock has advanced three times. Stephanie has finished characters and locations and is now on objects. Alex is working on locations. Paul just finished characters. Carl is still on characters. With only three hours left, Carl is in danger. The team needs to prioritize helping Carl interpret his visions. Remember: one stuck psychic ends the game for everyone.

walkthrough.title: The Beginner Mistake: Solved Players Disengaging
walkthrough.text: Stephanie finishes her third card at hour five and places her intuition token on the mission accomplished space. She gains two bonus clairvoyancy spaces for the two hours remaining. But instead of staying engaged, she leans back and checks her phone. This is a mistake! Solved psychics are incredibly valuable. They can study other psychics' visions without the stress of their own puzzle, offer fresh perspectives, and use remaining clairvoyancy tokens to place smart bets. Stay involved until the end.

walkthrough.title: Finishing Phase One
walkthrough.text: With the team's help, Carl finally solves his last card at hour six. Everyone breathes a sigh of relief. All four psychics have completed their character, location, and object combinations with one hour to spare. We now advance to Phase two, Revealing the Culprit. Each psychic removes their three cards from their sleeves and groups them on the table with numbered tokens.

walkthrough.title: The Shared Vision
walkthrough.text: Laura picks three vision cards from her hand. One should point to the character in the culprit's group, one to the location, and one to the object. She shuffles them and places them face down. She also secretly places the matching culprit token on the epilogue board. The psychics do not know which card corresponds to which element. This is the ghost's last chance to communicate, so every detail in those three cards matters.

walkthrough.title: Voting Based on Clairvoyancy
walkthrough.text: The first shared vision card is flipped. Stephanie, who has the lowest clairvoyancy score, must vote now with only this one card to go on. She slides a numbered token into her sleeve. The second card is revealed, and Alex and Carl vote. Finally, the third card is shown and Paul votes with full information. This staggered reveal is why the clairvoyancy track matters so much. Every space on that track means more information when it counts the most.

walkthrough.title: The Big Reveal
walkthrough.text: All votes are collected and revealed. Three out of four psychics chose group two. Laura flips the culprit token, and it shows the number two. The team wins! The ghost's spirit is finally at peace. If the votes had been wrong, the mystery would remain unsolved and we would need to try again next Samhain. Congratulations on completing your first game of Mysterium!

walkthrough.title: Before You Begin Your Own Game
walkthrough.text:
Before you start your own seance, here are three things to remember.

First, check out General Tips for advice on reading vision cards and playing the ghost effectively.

Second, reference the Appendix for a quick guide to components and game terms.

And most importantly, ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. Good luck solving the mystery!

bullets: Check out General Tips → ghost and psychic strategy basics
Reference the Appendix → quick-reference for components and terms
Ask your personal GM Guide → tap Q&A for instant answers during play

walkthrough.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
walkthrough.text: Let's cover the biggest pitfalls before diving into strategy. First, psychics reading vision cards too literally instead of looking for color, mood, and abstract connections. Second, the ghost overloading a psychic with too many vision cards, creating confusion instead of clarity. Third, psychics ignoring accumulated vision cards from previous rounds and only looking at the newest one. Fourth, hoarding clairvoyancy tokens instead of betting early and often. Fifth, solved psychics disengaging when their help could save a struggling teammate. Keep these in mind and you will avoid the most common traps.

walkthrough.title: Tips for the Ghost
walkthrough.text: As the ghost, your job is to find connections between your vision cards and the hidden assignments. Focus on the strongest visual link in each card, even if it is a small detail in the corner. One clear card is almost always better than three vague ones. When choosing cards, think about what will stand out to the psychic, not what makes sense to you. Pay attention to which psychics are behind on the clock and prioritize sending your clearest visions to them.

walkthrough.title: Tips for the Psychics
walkthrough.text: As a psychic, train yourself to look at vision cards from multiple angles. Start with color: does the dominant color match any suspect, location, or object card? Then look at mood and atmosphere. Then check small background details. Discuss your thinking out loud so other psychics can build on your observations. When you are stuck, ask yourself what the ghost might be trying to communicate rather than what the vision card literally shows.

walkthrough.title: Building a Shared Language
walkthrough.text: Over the course of the game, your group will develop a shared understanding of how the ghost communicates. Early rounds are about calibrating that language. If the ghost sends a card with a dominant blue tone to indicate a location with water, the psychics start to recognize that pattern. Pay attention to how the ghost thinks and what kinds of connections they tend to use. By mid-game, this shared language makes interpretation much faster and more accurate.

walkthrough.title: Clock Management
walkthrough.text: Seven hours sounds like a lot, but the clock moves quickly. Each psychic needs to solve three cards, which means three correct guesses minimum. If someone guesses wrong even once, they have used two hours on a single category. Keep constant track of who is behind and how many hours remain. The team should prioritize helping the slowest psychic, because one failure means everyone loses. It is better for three psychics to finish early and help the fourth than for everyone to barely scrape by alone.

walkthrough.title: Clairvoyancy Strategy
walkthrough.text: Your clairvoyancy tokens are free to use and cost you nothing if you are wrong. Place them whenever you have even a mild opinion about another psychic's guess. The upside of advancing on the clairvoyancy track is enormous. In the finale, psychics with high clairvoyancy see all three shared vision cards before voting, while those with low clairvoyancy see only one. That difference can be the margin between winning and losing the entire game.

walkthrough.title: The Finale Matters Most
walkthrough.text: Everything in Phase one is ultimately preparation for the final vote. Even if all psychics finish their three cards, you still need to correctly identify the culprit. This means the clairvoyancy track, which might seem like a side mechanic, is actually critical. It also means the ghost should save their best crow marker discard for Phase two if possible, since the shared vision is the single most important communication of the entire game.

walkthrough.title: First Game Ghost Selection
walkthrough.text: The ghost cannot speak, gesture, or react to the psychics' discussion. This requires patience and careful card selection. A new player in the ghost role may feel frustrated by the silence and make poor card choices under pressure. An experienced player who understands the game's visual language and can patiently sift through abstract connections will give the whole table a much better experience.

Always consider ALL accumulated vision cards, not just the newest
Use clairvoyancy tokens early — hoarding wastes free opportunities
Solved psychics: stay engaged and help struggling players

1 clear card almost always better than 3 vague ones

Discuss your thinking out loud so others can build on it

When stuck, look at all vision cards together for a combined message

Track patterns: color associations, mood cues, recurring themes
By mid-game, shared language speeds up interpretation
Each ghost develops their own 'accent' — learn it quickly

Track who is behind and how many hours remain constantly

Better for 3 to finish early and help 1 than all barely scrape by

summary.title: Clairvoyancy Strategy

High clairvoyancy = see all 3 shared vision cards in finale

Bet early and often to maximize track advancement

summary.title: The Finale Matters Most
bullets: Phase 1 is preparation for the final vote
Clairvoyancy track is critical, not a side mechanic
Ghost: save a crow discard for Phase 2 if possible
Shared vision = most important communication of the game

summary.title: First Game Ghost Selection
bullets: Choose an experienced, patient player for the ghost role
Ghost cannot speak, gesture, or react — requires discipline
New players may feel frustrated by silence under pressure
Good ghost = better experience for the whole table

walkthrough.title: Ghost: Card Selection Philosophy
walkthrough.text: Advanced ghost play is about maximizing separation between the correct answer and all the wrong answers. When you look at your hand, do not just find a card that matches the target. Find a card that matches the target and specifically does not match the other options. If two suspect cards both feature dark clothing, sending a dark-toned vision card is ambiguous. Instead, find a detail that uniquely identifies the correct suspect. The best clue is one that clearly points in one direction only.

walkthrough.title: Ghost: Reading Your Psychics
walkthrough.text: Every psychic interprets vision cards differently. Some think in colors, some in objects, some in emotions. Pay attention to how each psychic discusses the cards during the game. If Alex always notices colors first, send color-based clues to Alex. If Stephanie focuses on small objects, use detail-based connections for her. The ghost's silence is a constraint, but observation is a superpower. Tailor your communication style to each psychic's interpretation pattern.

walkthrough.title: Ghost: Hand Management
walkthrough.text: You draw back to seven cards after serving each psychic, which means the order in which you serve psychics matters. If your current hand has a perfect card for Carl but nothing good for Alex, serve Carl first while you have that card. After drawing new cards, you might find something better for Alex. Also consider saving your crow marker discard for moments when multiple psychics are stuck simultaneously, rather than using it for just one psychic.

walkthrough.title: Psychic: Process of Elimination
walkthrough.text: Advanced psychics use elimination, not just identification. When you receive a vision card, do not just look for the best match. Actively rule out options. If the vision card is overwhelmingly warm-toned, eliminate every cold-toned suspect. If it shows indoor imagery, rule out outdoor locations. By narrowing the field systematically, you can often reduce seven options down to two or three before committing. This approach is especially powerful when combined with accumulated vision cards from previous rounds.

walkthrough.title: Psychic: Cross-Reading Other Players' Visions
walkthrough.text: Each psychic has a unique hidden assignment. If you can figure out what another psychic's target is, you can eliminate that option from your own list. Watch what vision cards other psychics receive and listen to their discussions. If it seems obvious that Alex's character is the governess, you can cross that off your own list. This indirect information is free and powerful, especially when you are stuck between two similar options.

walkthrough.title: Clairvoyancy as Team Intelligence
walkthrough.text: Think of clairvoyancy tokens not just as personal advancement tools but as team intelligence signals. When you place an agree token, you are telling the table you have confidence in that guess. When you disagree, you are flagging a potential mistake. This meta-information helps the group calibrate confidence levels across all the guesses before the timer runs out. Coordinating clairvoyancy placement can elevate the whole team's accuracy, not just your own track position.

walkthrough.title: Phase Two: Ghost Strategy for the Shared Vision
walkthrough.text: The shared vision is the hardest communication challenge in the game because three cards must point to three different elements of the same group, and the psychics do not know which card maps to which element. Advanced ghosts think about this problem in advance and set aside promising vision cards during Phase one. If you know which group will be the culprit by hour four or five, start mentally bookmarking cards that could serve the finale. Use your crow marker discard here if your hand is weak.

walkthrough.title: Phase Two: Psychic Voting Strategy
walkthrough.text: When voting in the finale, do not just go with your gut. Analyze each shared vision card systematically against every group. Ask yourself: which character, location, and object does this card most likely reference? If you see all three cards, try to assign each card to an element and see which group consistently emerges. If you see fewer cards, lean on the strongest connection you can identify and trust the psychics voting after you to have more information. Communicate any insights you have before the staggered voting begins.

summary.title: Ghost: Card Selection Philosophy
bullets: Maximize separation: pick cards that match the target AND exclude wrong answers
Ambiguous clues (match multiple options) waste turns
Best clue = points in one direction only
Ask: does this card also match a wrong answer?

summary.title: Ghost: Reading Your Psychics
bullets: Each psychic interprets differently: color vs. objects vs. emotions
Listen to discussions to learn each psychic's interpretation style
Tailor clue type to each psychic's strength
Observation is the ghost's superpower despite silence

summary.title: Ghost: Hand Management
bullets: Serve psychics in order that maximizes card usage
Draw after each psychic → new cards may unlock solutions for others
Save crow discard for when multiple psychics are stuck
Consider reserving cards for Phase 2 shared vision

summary.title: Psychic: Process of Elimination
bullets: Rule out non-matches rather than only looking for best match
Warm vision card → eliminate cold-toned options
Accumulated vision cards help narrow 7 options to 2-3
Elimination is more reliable than positive identification

summary.title: Psychic: Cross-Reading Other Players' Visions
bullets: Each psychic has a unique target — overlap = at least 1 is wrong
Watch other psychics' vision cards and discussions
If another psychic's target is obvious, cross it off your list
Free indirect information, especially when stuck between 2 options

summary.title: Clairvoyancy as Team Intelligence
bullets: Agree/disagree tokens signal confidence to the whole table
Coordinated placement helps calibrate team accuracy
Meta-information layer on top of personal track advancement
Group-level clairvoyancy strategy > individual betting

summary.title: Phase Two: Ghost Strategy for the Shared Vision
bullets: Mentally bookmark strong vision cards during Phase 1
Know your culprit group early → plan shared vision cards
Save crow discard for Phase 2 if possible
3 cards must point to 3 different elements of the same group

summary.title: Phase Two: Psychic Voting Strategy
bullets: Analyze each shared vision card against every group systematically
Try to assign each card to character/location/object
With fewer cards: lean on strongest connection + trust later voters
Communicate insights before staggered voting begins

entries.definition: The player who knows the hidden assignments and communicates only through vision cards. The ghost cannot speak, gesture, or react during the game. One player is always the ghost.

All non-ghost players. Psychics receive vision cards, interpret them, discuss hypotheses openly, and place intuition tokens on their guesses. Each psychic has a unique hidden combination of suspect, location, and object to identify.

One of 84 surreal, abstract artwork cards used by the ghost to communicate. The ghost places one or more face-up in front of a psychic as clues. Connections may be based on color, mood, shapes, objects, or tiny background details.
One of 54 cards showing characters (suspects), locations, or objects. Laid face-up on the table as the options psychics choose from. Numbered on the back to match ghost cards behind the screen.
The matching card kept behind the ghost's screen for each psychic card in play. Numbers correspond to psychic card numbers. The ghost draws one per psychic per category to form hidden combinations.

A folding screen that hides the ghost's assignments. The interior has columns (one per psychic) and rows (character, location, object) where ghost cards are placed. Ghost tokens sit at the bottom of each column.

A colored token each psychic uses to mark their guess. Place it on the psychic card you believe matches the ghost's clue. Moves along the progress boards as you solve each category. Also used to vote in the two-to-three-player variant.
A colored card holder matching each psychic's color. When a psychic correctly identifies a card, they slide it into their sleeve for safekeeping. Also used to hold secret votes during the finale.

Tracks the current hour from I to VII. The hour hand advances once per round after the ghost reveals results. If any psychic has not finished all three categories by the end of hour VII, the team loses.

A two-minute timer the ghost flips after distributing all vision cards. Psychics must place their intuition tokens and clairvoyancy bets before it runs out. Discussion continues freely during the timer.

Four boards tracking each psychic's advancement: Character, Location, Object, and Epilogue. Psychics move from one board to the next as they correctly identify each category. The Epilogue board holds the mission accomplished and culprit spaces.

Double-sided tokens (agree/disagree) that psychics place on other psychics' guesses. Correct bets advance your position on the clairvoyancy track. Tokens are discarded after use and returned at the start of hour four. Not used in the two-to-three-player variant.

A scoring track that determines how many shared vision cards a psychic sees before voting in Phase two. Low level sees one card, intermediate sees two, high level sees all three. Advanced by correct clairvoyancy bets and finishing early.

Colored tokens matching each psychic. The ghost pushes a token against the screen after giving that psychic their vision cards, as a reminder. In Phase two, ghost tokens are numbered and used to label suspect groups.

Tokens that allow the ghost to discard their entire hand and redraw seven fresh vision cards. The number of crow markers depends on difficulty: Easy allows one discard per turn, Medium three per game, Hard one per game.

A numbered token the ghost places face-down on the epilogue board during Phase two to secretly designate which suspect group is the true culprit. Revealed at the end of the straw poll to determine if the team wins.

The main phase of the game, lasting up to seven hours. Each hour, the ghost sends vision cards and psychics guess their character, location, or object. Psychics must complete all three categories before the clock runs out.

The finale, triggered only if all psychics finish Phase one in time. Consists of three steps: suspect lineup, shared vision, and straw poll. The team wins if the majority vote matches the culprit token.

Three vision cards selected by the ghost in Phase two that point to the character, location, and object of the culprit's group. Shuffled and placed face-down, then revealed one at a time based on clairvoyancy levels. The ghost's final and most important communication.

The final majority vote in Phase two. Psychics vote secretly using numbered tokens based on their clairvoyancy level. Low-level psychics vote first with less information. The group with the most votes is the team's accusation.

The step within each hour where the ghost reveals whether each psychic guessed correctly or incorrectly. Correct psychics advance; incorrect psychics keep their vision cards and stay on the same progress board.

A space on the epilogue progress board where a psychic places their intuition token after solving all three categories. That psychic gains one clairvoyancy track space per remaining hour on the clock and continues helping other psychics.
Easy, Medium, or Hard. Determines the number of psychic cards laid out per category (more cards equals harder) and how many crow marker discards the ghost gets. Easy is recommended for first games.

Special rules for small groups. Each player controls two psychics. The clairvoyancy system is removed entirely. In Phase two, two random extra groups are added and the shared vision cards are placed face-up. Voting is open rather than secret.

The set of three cards (one character, one location, one object) secretly assigned to each psychic by the ghost. Each psychic has a unique combination that they must correctly identify during Phase one.
